title: EDHOC: A_3 external_aad missing CRED per RFC 9528 Section 4.4.2
description:
PROTOCOL: A_3 AEAD additional data construction missing CRED, breaking RFC 9528 compliance.

RFC 9528 Section 4.4.2 specifies:
```
A = ["Encrypt0", h'', TH_3 || CRED_I || ?EAD_3]
```

The external_aad (3rd element) must be the concatenation of TH_3 and CRED_I.

Current code (initiator lines 706-714, responder lines 1073-1084):
```c
zcbor_bstr_encode_ptr(zse_a3, ctx->th_3, 32)  // Only TH_3, missing CRED_I!
```

Impact:
- EDHOC sessions will NOT interoperate with RFC-compliant implementations
- The implementation currently works only because both initiator and responder have the same bug
- External implementations will fail AEAD decryption of Message 3

Fix: Build external_aad as concatenation of TH_3 and CRED_I:
```c
uint8_t ext_aad[64];  // TH_3 (32) + CRED_I (32 for raw pubkey)
memcpy(ext_aad, ctx->th_3, 32);
memcpy(ext_aad + 32, ctx->ed_pubkey, 32);  // CRED_I
zcbor_bstr_encode_ptr(zse_a3, ext_aad, 64);
```

Related: This supersedes/clarifies project-LICHEN-8idy which identified the structure but not the root cause.
